args: {"wheel":"quality","worktree":"/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent","branch":"bot/outline-agent-box","progress_file":"/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/WHEEL_PROGRESS.md","max_rounds":"6","mission":"Beat the DeepResearch-Bench RACE scoreboard by raising POLARIS report QUALITY. The remaining blocker is that RACE quality is UNSTABLE: two runs with BYTE-IDENTICAL config on the SAME corpus scored 0.4447 (step3: 1 outline turn, 3875 words, 11 rich sections including a 'Wage Inequality/Skill Demand' section (470w) and a 'Policy Implications' section (157w), 37 refs) versus 0.3518 (step5: 12 outline turns, 2901 words, only 8 rendered sections — it DROPPED the Wage-Inequality and Policy themes, thinned every body section, and bloated the bibliography to 52 refs). Config parity is byte-identical (reliability-wheel-proven: corpus/RQ/tier-fractions/model/judge all equal), so the whole 0.093 RACE gap is PURE agentic-outline NON-DETERMINISM: more turns produced a WORSE, thinner report. GOAL: make the agentic outline reliably produce the RICH, COMPLETE structure every run so the RACE FLOOR rises to >=0.4447 and climbs toward SOTA (top-3 deep-research systems ~0.45-0.50). The fix MUST be GENERAL (query-agnostic — derive themes from the corpus, never hardcode task-72 sections) and faithfulness is the ONLY hard gate and is NEVER relaxed (strict_verify + NLI + [#calc] lane + fold-in seam stay intact).","success_metric":"RACE Overall on DeepResearch-Bench task 72 >= 0.4447 REPRODUCIBLY across >=2 clean renders (proving the FLOOR rose, not a lucky draw), each with cp4_used=agentic and faithfulness_pass=true, AND the same fix re-validated on a 2nd DRB task to prove it is general (not task-72 overfit). Artifacts to study: outputs/step3_control/report.md (RACE 0.4447, 3875w, 11 sections, 37 refs) and outputs/step5_clean_credfix/report.md (RACE 0.3518, 2901w, 8 sections, 52 refs) — BOTH rendered from the SAME corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json (329 baskets) with byte-identical config. The delta: step5 ran 12 agentic outline turns and CONSOLIDATED the section plan down (8 vs 11 sections), silently dropping the Wage-Inequality(470w) and Policy(157w) themes and padding refs. RACE scorer: `set -a; . ./.env; set +a` (semicolons NOT '&&' — an unquoted-space line PG_EXA_CATEGORY breaks '&&' chaining) then `python scripts/score_report_race.py --report <report.md> --task-id 72 --model-name <name>` (prints Overall + 4 dims; ~3-5min). Full render (~43-47min): `python scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py --corpus data/cp4_corpus_s3gear_329.corrected.json --out-dir outputs/<name> --rq-drb-task 72`. Faithfulness engine (DO NOT TOUCH): strict_verify + NLI entailment_judge + verified [#calc] lane + fold-in seam.","next_actions":"ROUND 1 = DIAGNOSIS + FIX-DESIGN ONLY, NO expensive render yet. (1) READ the real code: the agentic outline multi-turn loop in src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py and the section-planning + basket->section routing + keep/drop + reference assembly in src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py. Find the exact MECHANISM by which 12 turns collapse 11 rich sections into 8 thin ones and drop whole themes (does the loop over-consolidate the outline each turn? merge sibling themes? drop baskets below a score? pad refs?). Diff the two runs' outline/section plans if the artifacts capture them. (2) DESIGN a GENERAL fix that GUARANTEES the final section plan covers ALL major corpus themes as DISTINCT, SUBSTANTIAL sections regardless of how many agentic turns run (no theme silently merged/dropped by non-determinism), and caps reference-section bloat — raising the RACE floor. It must derive the theme set from the corpus (query-agnostic), and must NOT weaken faithfulness. Land the code, exercise it cheaply where possible (unit-level / dry section-plan without a full render). Give Fable the mechanism + the designed fix to gate. ONLY after Fable gates the fix design as correct+general+faithfulness-safe, ROUND 2: run ONE full render + RACE-score to validate >=0.4447; if it beats, run a 2nd render (reproducibility) and a 2nd DRB task (generality). Commit every step with real numbers. Do NOT overfit to task 72. Do NOT touch the faithfulness engine. Do NOT chase speed."}

summary: Opus-grinds / Fable-scalpel wheel: Opus does ALL hard work (read, search, build, fix, measure) toward a benchmark target; Fable is called ONCE per round for a precise deep-think gate only — never for hard read/search. Loops until Fable gates it top.

phases.title: Work
phases.detail: Opus: read the named files, apply the next concrete step, exercise it, commit, MEASURE the benchmark

phases.title: Gate
phases.detail: Fable: precise deep-think on the diff + measurement — correct? faithfulness-safe? top yet? exact next step
